nodes.title: Payments latency spike
nodes.summary: P95 payments latency returned below 1.1 seconds after rollback, reroute, and the retry-cap rollout.

nodes.title: Retry storm amplified load
nodes.summary: The retry storm was confirmed as the secondary amplifier, and concurrency normalized after the retry-cap rollout.

nodes.title: Apply retry cap
nodes.summary: Retry cap of 2 with full jitter was rolled out across payments clients.

relations.rationale: Concurrency fell back toward baseline after the retry cap rollout, confirming retries amplified the degraded pool.
relations.evidence: post-mitigation concurrency dashboard plus application logs

relations.rationale: The mitigation task was executed to stop client amplification while rollback and reroute completed.
relations.method: Retry cap set to 2 with full jitter in shared client configuration

node_details.detail: The retry storm remained the secondary amplifier of the incident. After the retry-cap rollout, request concurrency fell back toward baseline and queue growth stopped.
The retry-cap change was completed with limit 2 and full jitter. Request concurrency and DB wait time fell back toward normal within minutes.
